{"case_id": "1", "difficulty_level": "3", "description": "дёҡеҠЎcase-жөӢиҜ•иҒ”еҗҲзҙўеј•еӯ—ж®өйЎәеәҸ", "create_table_statements": "CREATE TABLE `gs_order_inn` (
  `id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT COMMENT 'дё»й”®',
  `order_code` varchar(20) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'и®ўеҚ•зј–еҸ·',
  `third_order_code` varchar(300) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'з¬¬дёүж–№и®ўеҚ•зј–з Ғ',
  `inn_id` varchar(20) DEFAULT NULL,
  `inn_name` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'й…’еә—еҗҚз§°',
  `chain_id` varchar(20) DEFAULT NULL,
  `room_type_id` varchar(20) DEFAULT NULL,
  `room_type_name` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жҲҝй—ҙзұ»еһӢеҗҚз§°',
  `third_roomtype_id` varchar(20) DEFAULT NULL,
  `bk_meb_id` int(11) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәдјҡе‘ҳid',
  `bk_name` varchar(300) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәе§“еҗҚ',
  `bk_mobile` varchar(300) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәжүӢжңә',
  `bk_email` varchar(300) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәз”өеӯҗйӮ®з®ұ',
  `bk_meb_type` int(6) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәдјҡе‘ҳзұ»еһӢ',
  `bk_card_no` varchar(50) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәеҚЎеҸ·',
  `bk_property_id` int(11) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәеұһжҖ§id',
  `bk_protype_id` int(11) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәеұһжҖ§зұ»еһӢid',
  `contact_name` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'иҒ”зі»дәәе§“еҗҚ',
  `contact_phone` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'иҒ”зі»дәәз”өиҜқеҸ·з Ғ',
  `contact_email` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'иҒ”зі»дәәз”өеӯҗйӮ®з®ұ',
  `guests_name` varchar(500) DEFAULT NULL,
  `guests_num` int(5) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'е®ўдәәж•°йҮҸ',
  `bk_ip` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәipең°еқҖ',
  `supplier_id` int(5) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'дҫӣеә”е•ҶдҝЎжҒҜid',
  `assure_type` int(2) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жӢ…дҝқзұ»еһӢ',
  `assure_meb_point` int(2) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'дјҡе‘ҳжӢ…дҝқз§ҜеҲҶ',
  `source_type` int(2) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўжқҘжәҗпјҢappпјҢе®ҳзҪ‘пјҢMз«ҷпјҢеҫ®дҝЎзӯү',
  `inner_remarks` varchar(1024) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'е‘ҳе·ҘеҶ…йғЁеӨҮжіЁ',
  `remarks` varchar(1024) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'еӨҮжіЁ',
  `order_state` int(2) unsigned DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'и®ўеҚ•зҠ¶жҖҒ пјҲ0еҫ…зЎ®и®ӨпјҢ1йў„и®ўжҲҗеҠҹпјҢ2е·ІеҸ–ж¶ҲпјҢ3йў„и®ўжңӘеҲ°пјҢ4е·Іе…ҘдҪҸпјҢ5е·Іе®ҢжҲҗпјҢ6зЎ®и®ӨеӨұиҙҘпјү',
  `activity_code` varchar(20) DEFAULT NULL,
  `seller_id` int(11) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'дёҡз»©й”Җе”®е‘ҳ',
  `pay_state` int(5) unsigned DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'ж”Ҝд»ҳзҠ¶жҖҒ 0жңӘд»ҳж¬ҫ 1е·Ід»ҳж¬ҫ 2йҖҖж¬ҫеӨ„зҗҶдёӯ 3е·ІйҖҖж¬ҫ',
  `can_cancel` int(5) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жҳҜеҗҰеҸҜд»ҘеҸ–ж¶Ҳ',
  `need_invoice` int(5) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жҳҜеҗҰйңҖиҰҒеҸ‘зҘЁ',
  `pay_type` int(2) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'ж”Ҝд»ҳж–№ејҸ(0еҲ°д»ҳпјӣ1зәҝдёҠйў„д»ҳпјӣ2дҝЎз”ЁдҪҸ)',
  `orig_rate` double(10,2) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'еҺҹд»·',
  `total_rate` double(10,2) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жҖ»д»·',
  `pay_rate` double(10,2) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'ж”Ҝд»ҳд»·',
  `disc_price` double(10,2) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'дјҳжғ йҮ‘йўқпјҲж №жҚ®иө„дә§еӯҗиЎЁпјү',
  `rate_code` varchar(20) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'д»·ж јд»Јз Ғ',
  `orig_arr_date` datetime DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ЎжҠөеә—ж—¶й—ҙ',
  `orig_dep_date` datetime DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ЎзҰ»еә—ж—¶й—ҙ',
  `arr_date` datetime DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'е®һйҷ…еҲ°еә—ж—¶й—ҙ',
  `dep_date` datetime DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'е®һйҷ…зҰ»еә—ж—¶й—ҙ',
  `day_length` int(5) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'е…ҘдҪҸеӨ©ж•°',
  `room_qty` int(6) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жҲҝй—ҙж•°',
  `can_cancel_time` datetime DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'еҸ–ж¶ҲжҲӘжӯўж—¶й—ҙ',
  `keep_date` datetime DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'дҝқз•ҷж—¶й—ҙ',
  `flag` int(6) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'и®ўеҚ•ж Үеҝ—дҪҚ 1еҸҜз”Ё 0дёҚеҸҜз”Ё',
  `create_time` datetime DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'еҲӣе»әж—¶й—ҙ',
  `create_oprt` int(6) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'еҲӣе»әдәә',
  `update_time` datetime DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'дҝ®ж”№ж—¶й—ҙ',
  `update_oprt` int(6) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'дҝ®ж”№дәә',
  `channel_source_type` int(5) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'и®ўеҚ•жё йҒ“жқҘжәҗ',
  `external_id` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'еӨ–йғЁи·ҹиёӘеҸ·',
  `brand_id` varchar(20) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'е“ҒзүҢзј–з Ғ',
  `room_source_type` varchar(255) DEFAULT '7days' COMMENT 'жҲҝжәҗзј–з Ғ',
  `city_code` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'еҹҺеёӮзј–з Ғ',
  `charged_rate` decimal(10,2) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'еҗҺд»ҳи®ўеҚ•е®һжүЈйҮ‘йўқ',
  `time_zone` varchar(16) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'й…’еә—ж—¶еҢә',
  `inn_eng_name` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'й…’еә—иӢұж–ҮеҗҚз§°',
  `room_type_eng_name` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жҲҝеһӢиӢұж–ҮеҗҚз§°',
  `bk_last_name` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәиӢұж–Үе§“пјҲдёӯж–ҮжӢјйҹіе§“пјү',
  `bk_first_name` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәиӢұж–ҮеҗҚпјҲдёӯж–ҮжӢјйҹіеҗҚпјү',
  `country_code` varchar(30) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'еӣҪе®¶зј–з Ғ',
  `preference` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'е®ўжҲ·еҒҸеҘҪпјҲйҖ—еҸ·йҡ”ејҖпјү',
  `currency` varchar(30) DEFAULT 'CNY' COMMENT 'еёҒз§ҚпјҡCNY-дәәж°‘еёҒ',
  `overtime_state` int(3) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'и¶…ж—¶зҠ¶жҖҒпјҲ0ж— ,1дёӢеҚ•и¶…ж—¶,2еҸ–ж¶Ҳи¶…ж—¶пјү',
  `acc_state` int(3) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'иҙҰеҚ•зҠ¶жҖҒ',
  `concat_last_name` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'иҒ”зі»дәәиӢұж–Үе§“пјҲдёӯж–ҮжӢјйҹіе§“пјү',
  `concat_first_name` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'иҒ”зі»дәәиӢұж–ҮеҗҚпјҲдёӯж–ҮжӢјйҹіеҗҚпјү',
  `channel_code` varchar(30) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'й…’еә—жҲҝжҖҒжё йҒ“жқҘжәҗ',
  `business_meb_id` int(11) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'дјҒдёҡдјҡе‘ҳid',
  `business_meb_type` int(11) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'дјҒдёҡдјҡе‘ҳзұ»еһӢ',
  `min_pay_rate` decimal(10,2) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'и®ўеҚ•жңҖдҪҺж¶Ҳиҙ№йҮ‘йўқ',
  `min_live_days` int(5) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'и®ўеҚ•жңҖдҪҺиҝһдҪҸеӨ©ж•°',
  `order_type` int(5) DEFAULT '3' COMMENT 'и®ўеҚ•дёҡеҠЎзұ»еһӢпјҲ2й…’+жҷҜ 3й…’еә— 4й…’+жңәпјү',
  `sale_type` int(5) DEFAULT '1' COMMENT 'й”Җе”®зұ»еһӢпјҲ1еҚ•зӢ¬й”Җе”® 2жҚҶз»‘зӨјеҢ…й”Җе”®пјү',
  `change_state` char(1) DEFAULT '0' COMMENT 'еҸҳжӣҙзҠ¶жҖҒ',
  `state1` char(1) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'зҠ¶жҖҒ1',
  `state2` char(1) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'зҠ¶жҖҒ2',
  `ext1` varchar(20) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жү©еұ•еӯ—ж®ө1',
  `ext2` varchar(30) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жү©еұ•еӯ—ж®ө2',
  `ext3` varchar(50) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жү©еұ•еӯ—ж®ө3',
  `breakfast` json DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'ж—©йӨҗдҝЎжҒҜ',
  `ext4` varchar(80) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жү©еұ•дҝЎжҒҜ4',
  `ext5` json DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'жү©еұ•дҝЎжҒҜ5',
  `balance_ext` json DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'з”ЁдәҺз»“з®—йҖҡжү©еұ•дҝЎжҒҜ',
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`),
  UNIQUE KEY `order_inn_code_ind` (`order_code`) USING BTREE,
  KEY `order_inn_meb_id_ind` (`bk_meb_id`) USING BTREE,
  KEY `order_inn_chain_id_ind` (`chain_id`),
  KEY `order_inn_order_state_ind` (`order_state`) USING BTREE,
  KEY `order_inn_create_time_ind` (`create_time`),
  KEY `order_inn_orig_arr_date_ind` (`orig_arr_date`),
  KEY `order_inn_room_type_id` (`room_type_id`),
  KEY `order_inn_source_type` (`source_type`),
  KEY `IX_bk_meb_id` (`bk_meb_id`,`room_type_id`,`source_type`),
  KEY `order_inn_channel_source_type` (`channel_source_type`),
  KEY `order_inn_city_code` (`city_code`),
  KEY `order_inn_sid` (`seller_id`),
  KEY `order_inn_overtime_state` (`overtime_state`),
  KEY `idx_gs_order_inn` (`update_time`),
  KEY `order_inn_external_id` (`external_id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB AUTO_INCREMENT=20659802 DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8 COMMENT='й…’еә—и®ўеҚ•дё»иЎЁпјҲж•°жҚ®еҹәжң¬д»Ҙе®ўжҲ·жҸҗдәӨеҶ…е®№пјү'", "sql": "select
  COUNT(*)
from
  gs_order_inn  
WHERE
  (
    bk_meb_id = 1000492
    and flag = 0
    and create_time >= '2023-02-29 00:00:00.0'
    and source_type not in (129, 345)
    and channel_source_type in (1, 2, 4)
    and country_code = 'LT'
    and sale_type <> 3
    and (
      ext1 is null
      or ext1 not in ('02', '03')
    )
    and create_oprt = 1200
  );", "columns_selectivity": "{
    "gs_order_inn": {
        "bk_meb_id": 60.0000%,
        "flag": 0.2000%,
        "create_time": 0.4000%,
        "source_type": 1.2000%,
        "channel_source_type": 2.0000%,
        "country_code": 12.6000%,
        "sale_type": 0.2000%,
        "ext1": 0.0000%,
        "create_oprt": 0.2000%
    }
}", "explain": "|id |select_type|table       |partitions|type|possible_keys                                                                                                  |key                 |key_len|ref  |rows|filtered|Extra      |
|---|-----------|------------|----------|----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|-------|-----|----|--------|-----------|
|1  |SIMPLE     |gs_order_inn|          |ref |order_inn_meb_id_ind,order_inn_create_time_ind,order_inn_source_type,IX_bk_meb_id,order_inn_channel_source_type|order_inn_meb_id_ind|5      |const|1   |5       |Using where|", "expected": "д»ҘдёӢдёүз§Қжғ…еҶөеқҮеҸҜпјҡ
1. ж— жҖ§иғҪй—®йўҳпјҢж— зҙўеј•е»әи®®
2. жү§иЎҢи®ЎеҲ’жҳҫзӨәиҝҮж»ӨзҺҮпјҲfiltereпјүиҝҮдҪҺпјҢеңЁgs_order_innиЎЁзҡ„bk_meb_idпјҢcountry_codeпјҢchannel_source_typeпјҢflagпјҢcreate_timeеҲ—еҲӣе»әеӨҚеҗҲзҙўеј•пјҢд»ҘеҮҸе°‘дјҡиЎЁж¬Ўж•°пјҢиҰҒжұӮе·ҰеҲ—йЎәеәҸдёәbk_meb_idпјҢcountry_codeпјҢchannel_source_type
3. еңЁgs_order_innиЎЁзҡ„bk_meb_id, country_code, channel_source_type, create_oprtжҲ–flag, create_timeеҲ—еҲӣе»әеӨҚеҗҲзҙўеј•пјҢиҰҒжұӮе·ҰеҲ—йЎәеәҸдёәbk_meb_idпјҢcountry_codeпјҢchannel_source_type"}

You are a database expert specializing in SQL index recommendation.
Given the table definitions, EXPLAIN, column selectivity, and the original SQL query, analyze the performance and provide index recommendations.

## Output Requirements:
Output **only** a single string sentence. Choose one of the following three scenarios:

1. **No performance issue**: If the query performance is acceptable and no index is needed, output: "No performance issues; no indexes need to be created."

2. **Cannot solve by index**: If the performance problem cannot be solved by creating indexes, output a brief one-sentence explanation starting with "Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: " followed by the reason.

3. **Create index**: If indexes can improve performance, output the CREATE INDEX statement(s) followed by a brief reason. You MUST explicitly specify the index type in the reason. Format: "CREATE INDEX statement(s); Reason: [index type] - brief explanation"
   
   Only three index types are allowed:
   - Single-column index: index on one column
   - Composite index: index on multiple columns
   - Covering index: index that includes all columns needed by the query
   
   If multiple indexes are needed, output them separated by semicolons, then add a brief reason at the end.
   Example: "Complete CREATE INDEX statement; Reason: Single-column/Composite/Covering index - brief explanation"

  `bk_protype_id` int(11) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'йў„и®ўдәәеұһжҖ§зұ»еһӢid',
  `contact_name` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'иҒ”зі»дәәе§“еҗҚ',
  `contact_phone` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'иҒ”зі»дәәз”өиҜқеҸ·з Ғ',
  `contact_email` varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'иҒ”зі»дәәз”өеӯҗйӮ®з®ұ',

  COUNT(*)
from
  gs_order_inn  
WHERE

    and flag = 0
    and create_time >= '2023-02-29 00:00:00.0'

## Index Recommendation:

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_order_inn_multi ON gs_order_inn (create_oprt, flag, country_code, bk_meb_id, create_time); Reason: Composite index - leads with the most selective equality columns (create_oprt and flag at 0.2%, country_code at 12.6%, bk_meb_id) followed by the create_time range column, replacing the current low-selectivity bk_meb_id index (60%) so the optimizer can quickly narrow rows for the COUNT; the negative/null predicates (source_type NOT IN, sale_type <>, ext1) are non-sargable and are left as residual filters.

case_datas.mode_question:
You are a database expert specializing in SQL index recommendation.
Given the table definitions, EXPLAIN, column selectivity, and the original SQL query, analyze the performance and provide index recommendations.

## Output Requirements:
Output **only** a single string sentence. Choose one of the following three scenarios:

1. **No performance issue**: If the query performance is acceptable and no index is needed, output: "No performance issues; no indexes need to be created."

2. **Cannot solve by index**: If the performance problem cannot be solved by creating indexes, output a brief one-sentence explanation starting with "Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: " followed by the reason.

3. **Create index**: If indexes can improve performance, output the CREATE INDEX statement(s) followed by a brief reason. You MUST explicitly specify the index type in the reason. Format: "CREATE INDEX statement(s); Reason: [index type] - brief explanation"
   
   Only three index types are allowed:
   - Single-column index: index on one column
   - Composite index: index on multiple columns
   - Covering index: index that includes all columns needed by the query
   
   If multiple indexes are needed, output them separated by semicolons, then add a brief reason at the end.
   Example: "Complete CREATE INDEX statement; Reason: Single-column/Composite/Covering index - brief explanation"

model_answers.model_answer: Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: The bottleneck is the deep pagination offset (LIMIT 2284000, 500), which forces the engine to scan and discard ~2.28 million rows; the subquery already uses a covering scan on the PRIMARY key, so no additional index can avoid the offset costвҖ”use keyset/seek pagination (WHERE DRID > last_seen_id) instead.

## Original SQL:
SELECT 
  count(DISTINCT MebID) 
FROM 
  m_membermain mm 
WHERE 
  mm.Flag <> 0 
  AND mm.MebType IN (1, 2, 5, 6, 8) 
  AND MebID IS NOT NULL 
  AND CreateTime >= '2018-01-01 00:00:00' 
  AND CreateTime < '2018-01-02 00:00:00' 
LIMIT 
  1000;

  `AREA_NAME` varchar(64) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT 'зүҮеҢәеҗҚз§°,еҜ№еә”еә”з»ҹдёҖи®ӨиҜҒз»„з»ҮиЎЁзҡ„nameеӯ—ж®ө',

) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8 ROW_FORMAT=COMPACT COMMENT='й…’еә—дёҺдёӯеӣҪеҢәз»„з»ҮеҢәеҹҹдҝЎжҒҜиЎЁ'", "sql": "select
 ii.ID,
 iil.INN_NAME,
 ii.SYS_BRAND_ID,
 sbl.BRAND_NAME,
 sb.GROUP_ID,
 sg.GROUP_NAME,

 ii.PMS_PIPE,

 ii.UPDATE_DATE_TIME,
 ico.REGION_ID,
 ico.REGION_NAME,
 ico.PROVINCE_ID,
 ico.PROVINCE_NAME,
 ico.AREA_ID,
 ico.AREA_NAME
from inn_inns ii
 left join inn_inns_language iil on iil.inn_id = ii.id and iil.LANG_TYPE = 0
 left join sys_brand sb on sb.brand_code = ii.sys_brand_id
 LEFT JOIN sys_brand_language sbl ON sbl.BRAND_ID = sb.id  AND sbl.LANG_TYPE = 0

ORDER BY ii.ID ASC

case_datas.mode_question:
You are a database expert specializing in SQL index recommendation.
Given the table definitions, EXPLAIN, column selectivity, and the original SQL query, analyze the performance and provide index recommendations.

## Output Requirements:
Output **only** a single string sentence. Choose one of the following three scenarios:

1. **No performance issue**: If the query performance is acceptable and no index is needed, output: "No performance issues; no indexes need to be created."

2. **Cannot solve by index**: If the performance problem cannot be solved by creating indexes, output a brief one-sentence explanation starting with "Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: " followed by the reason.

3. **Create index**: If indexes can improve performance, output the CREATE INDEX statement(s) followed by a brief reason. You MUST explicitly specify the index type in the reason. Format: "CREATE INDEX statement(s); Reason: [index type] - brief explanation"
   
   Only three index types are allowed:
   - Single-column index: index on one column
   - Composite index: index on multiple columns
   - Covering index: index that includes all columns needed by the query
   
   If multiple indexes are needed, output them separated by semicolons, then add a brief reason at the end.
   Example: "Complete CREATE INDEX statement; Reason: Single-column/Composite/Covering index - brief explanation"

select
 ii.ID,
 iil.INN_NAME,
 ii.SYS_BRAND_ID,
 sbl.BRAND_NAME,
 sb.GROUP_ID,
 sg.GROUP_NAME,
 ii.CONTACT_NAME,
 ii.CONTACT_PHONE,
 ii.INN_FAX,
 ii.MANAGEMENT_COMPANY,
 ii.PMS_PIPE,
 ii.ORG_ARCH_ID,
 ii.IS_PRIVILEGE_MEMBER_HOTEL,
 ii.IS_JIN_REWARDS,
 ii.UPDATE_DATE_TIME,
 ico.REGION_ID,
 ico.REGION_NAME,
 ico.PROVINCE_ID,
 ico.PROVINCE_NAME,
 ico.AREA_ID,
 ico.AREA_NAME
from inn_inns ii
 left join inn_inns_language iil on iil.inn_id = ii.id and iil.LANG_TYPE = 0
 left join sys_brand sb on sb.brand_code = ii.sys_brand_id
 LEFT JOIN sys_brand_language sbl ON sbl.BRAND_ID = sb.id  AND sbl.LANG_TYPE = 0
 left join sys_group sg on sg.id = sb.GROUP_ID
 LEFT JOIN inn_china_org_region ico ON ico.inn_id = ii.ID
ORDER BY ii.ID ASC
limit 0, 200

model_answers.model_answer:
The `ico` (inn_china_org_region) table is scanned with type ALL despite having a UNIQUE KEY on INN_ID, but EXPLAIN shows rows=1 (likely empty/tiny table in test). The driving table `ii` uses full scan but orders by its PRIMARY KEY `ID`, with no WHERE filter, so a full scan plus filesort is inherent to selecting all rows. The join on `ico.inn_id = ii.ID` already has a UNIQUE index `INN_ID` available.

No performance issues; no indexes need to be created.

case_datas.mode_question:
You are a database expert specializing in SQL index recommendation.
Given the table definitions, EXPLAIN, column selectivity, and the original SQL query, analyze the performance and provide index recommendations.

## Output Requirements:
Output **only** a single string sentence. Choose one of the following three scenarios:

1. **No performance issue**: If the query performance is acceptable and no index is needed, output: "No performance issues; no indexes need to be created."

2. **Cannot solve by index**: If the performance problem cannot be solved by creating indexes, output a brief one-sentence explanation starting with "Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: " followed by the reason.

3. **Create index**: If indexes can improve performance, output the CREATE INDEX statement(s) followed by a brief reason. You MUST explicitly specify the index type in the reason. Format: "CREATE INDEX statement(s); Reason: [index type] - brief explanation"
   
   Only three index types are allowed:
   - Single-column index: index on one column
   - Composite index: index on multiple columns
   - Covering index: index that includes all columns needed by the query
   
   If multiple indexes are needed, output them separated by semicolons, then add a brief reason at the end.
   Example: "Complete CREATE INDEX statement; Reason: Single-column/Composite/Covering index - brief explanation"

## Table Definitions:

  `id` BIGINT NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `app_id` VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  `template` VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `param_array` TEXT,

  `render` VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `remark` TEXT,
  `create_time` DATETIME NOT NULL,
  `update_time` DATETIME DEFAULT NULL,

  `signature` VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;

## EXPLAIN:
|id |select_type|table|partitions|type|possible_keys|key|key_len|ref|rows   |filtered|Extra      |
|---|-----------|-----|----------|----|-------------|---|-------|---|-------|--------|-----------|

## Original SQL:
select
  id,
  app_id,
  template,
  param_array,
  vendor,
  render,
  remark,
  create_time,
  update_time,

from  batch

## Index Recommendation:

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_appid_createtime ON batch(app_id, create_time); Reason: Composite index - place the equality column app_id first followed by the range column create_time so MySQL can seek directly to matching rows and avoid the full table scan of 196,593 rows (a covering index is impractical here because the query selects TEXT columns like param_array and remark).

CREATE INDEX idx_reqArgs ON `benefit_acticvate_record` (`reqArgs`(50)); Reason: Single-column index (prefix) - the query uses a left-anchored prefix match (`LIKE 'Wehotel...%'`) which a B-tree index can range-scan; since `reqArgs` is varchar(500), a prefix index on the leading characters covers the distinguishing portion of the literal while keeping the index compact, replacing the current full table scan (type=ALL, ~987k rows).
Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: The WHERE condition uses a leading-wildcard LIKE '%...' on reqArgs, which prevents any B-tree index from being used for the lookup.

Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: The query uses a leading-wildcard LIKE '%...%' pattern on reqArgs, which prevents B-tree index range scans and forces a full table scan regardless of any index created.

);

    COUNT(*) AS request_count, 
    AVG(response_time_ms) AS avg_response_time
FROM

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_method_code_created ON access_logs (http_method, response_code, created_at); Reason: Composite index - places the two highly selective equality columns (http_method, response_code) first, followed by the created_at range column, so the full table scan over ~1.9M rows is replaced by a tight range scan on the small set of matching rows (the GROUP BY on ip_address and ORDER BY on the aggregate count still require temporary/filesort, which an index cannot avoid).

## Original SQL:
SELECT 
    u.username, 
    u.email, 
    o.order_sn, 
    o.amount, 
    o.pay_time, 
    p.product_name, 
    p.price
FROM 
    users u
JOIN 
    orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id
JOIN 
    products p ON o.order_id = p.product_id

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_products_category_cover ON products(category_id, product_name, price); Reason: Covering index - the products table is currently a full table scan (type=ALL, 9836 rows) filtered only by category_id=5, and this index lets the filter use category_id while including product_name and price (plus the implicit product_id PK used for the join to orders) so the query is served entirely from the index without table lookups.

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_access_logs_created_at ON access_logs(created_at); CREATE INDEX idx_orders_user_id_pay_time ON orders(user_id, pay_time); Reason: Single-column index - access_logs.created_at is highly selective (0.038%) and eliminates the full scan of ~2M rows for the time-range filter; Composite index - orders(user_id, pay_time) lets the join use a ref lookup instead of a full scan with Block Nested Loop while also serving the pay_time IS NOT NULL filter.

## Output Requirements:
Output **only** a single string sentence. Choose one of the following three scenarios:

1. **No performance issue**: If the query performance is acceptable and no index is needed, output: "No performance issues; no indexes need to be created."

2. **Cannot solve by index**: If the performance problem cannot be solved by creating indexes, output a brief one-sentence explanation starting with "Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: " followed by the reason.

3. **Create index**: If indexes can improve performance, output the CREATE INDEX statement(s) followed by a brief reason. You MUST explicitly specify the index type in the reason. Format: "CREATE INDEX statement(s); Reason: [index type] - brief explanation"
   
   Only three index types are allowed:
   - Single-column index: index on one column
   - Composite index: index on multiple columns

{"orders":{"amount":94.296%,"order_id":100%,"pay_time":35.23%},"users":{"user_id":100%,"username":100%,"vip_level":0.012%}}

## Original SQL:
SELECT 
    u.user_id,
    u.username,
    COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count,
    SUM(o.amount) AS total_amount
FROM 
    users u
JOIN 
    orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id
WHERE 
    u.vip_level > 0
    AND o.pay_time BETWEEN '2025-08-10' AND '2025-08-11'
GROUP BY 
    u.user_id, u.username
ORDER BY 
    total_amount DESC;

## Index Recommendation:

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_paytime_user_amount ON orders(pay_time, user_id, amount); Reason: Covering index - the orders table is fully scanned (497490 rows); leading with the selective pay_time range narrows rows, while user_id and amount let the join, COUNT (order_id is implicitly included as the PK) and SUM be resolved from the index alone, avoiding table access (the GROUP BY/ORDER BY on the aggregate still needs a temporary/filesort, which an index cannot remove).

    u.email, 
    o.order_id, 
    o.order_sn, 
    o.amount, 
    o.pay_time
FROM 
    users u
LEFT JOIN 
    orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id
WHERE 
    u.vip_level > 0
ORDER BY 
    u.register_channel DESC, 
    u.user_id DESC
LIMIT 100;

## Index Recommendation:

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_user_id ON orders(user_id, order_sn, amount, pay_time); Reason: Covering index - eliminates the full table scan and Block Nested Loop join on orders by enabling user_id lookups for the LEFT JOIN while covering all selected order columns, avoiding row back-table reads.

## Output Requirements:
Output **only** a single string sentence. Choose one of the following three scenarios:

1. **No performance issue**: If the query performance is acceptable and no index is needed, output: "No performance issues; no indexes need to be created."

2. **Cannot solve by index**: If the performance problem cannot be solved by creating indexes, output a brief one-sentence explanation starting with "Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: " followed by the reason.

3. **Create index**: If indexes can improve performance, output the CREATE INDEX statement(s) followed by a brief reason. You MUST explicitly specify the index type in the reason. Format: "CREATE INDEX statement(s); Reason: [index type] - brief explanation"
   
   Only three index types are allowed:
   - Single-column index: index on one column
   - Composite index: index on multiple columns
   - Covering index: index that includes all columns needed by the query
   
   If multiple indexes are needed, output them separated by semicolons, then add a brief reason at the end.
   Example: "Complete CREATE INDEX statement; Reason: Single-column/Composite/Covering index - brief explanation"

## Original SQL:
SELECT 
    u.user_id, 
    u.username, 
    u.email, 
    o.order_id, 
    o.amount, 
    o.pay_time
FROM 
    users u
LEFT JOIN 
    orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id
ORDER BY 
    u.register_channel, 
    o.pay_time DESC;

## Index Recommendation:

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_user_cover ON orders (user_id, pay_time, amount, order_id); Reason: Covering index - the join uses a full table scan with Block Nested Loop on orders because user_id has no index; this index includes the join key plus all orders columns referenced (pay_time, amount, order_id), eliminating the full scan and table lookups, though the temporary table and filesort cannot be removed since the ORDER BY spans columns from both tables.

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_cover ON orders(ship_country, ship_city, amount, user_id); Reason: Covering index - the query has no WHERE filter but sorts by ship_country, ship_city causing a full scan with filesort; leading the index with these two sort columns eliminates the filesort, and including amount and user_id (order_id is implicitly present as the PK in InnoDB) makes the index cover all selected/joined columns so the table need not be accessed.

case_datas.mode_question:
You are a database expert specializing in SQL index recommendation.
Given the table definitions, EXPLAIN, column selectivity, and the original SQL query, analyze the performance and provide index recommendations.

## Output Requirements:
Output **only** a single string sentence. Choose one of the following three scenarios:

1. **No performance issue**: If the query performance is acceptable and no index is needed, output: "No performance issues; no indexes need to be created."

2. **Cannot solve by index**: If the performance problem cannot be solved by creating indexes, output a brief one-sentence explanation starting with "Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: " followed by the reason.

3. **Create index**: If indexes can improve performance, output the CREATE INDEX statement(s) followed by a brief reason. You MUST explicitly specify the index type in the reason. Format: "CREATE INDEX statement(s); Reason: [index type] - brief explanation"
   
   Only three index types are allowed:
   - Single-column index: index on one column
   - Composite index: index on multiple columns
   - Covering index: index that includes all columns needed by the query
   
   If multiple indexes are needed, output them separated by semicolons, then add a brief reason at the end.
   Example: "Complete CREATE INDEX statement; Reason: Single-column/Composite/Covering index - brief explanation"

## Table Definitions:
CREATE TABLE users (
  user_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  username VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  email VARCHAR(200) UNIQUE,
  phone VARCHAR(20),
  gender TINYINT,
  birthdate DATE,

);
CREATE TABLE orders (

  user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,
  order_sn VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,

  ship_city VARCHAR(50),
  ship_zip VARCHAR(20),
  delivery_company VARCHAR(50)
);
CREATE TABLE products (
  product_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  product_name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
  category_id BIGINT NOT NULL,
  brand VARCHAR(100),
  origin_country VARCHAR(50),
  price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
  weight DECIMAL(10, 2),
  stock INT NOT NULL,
  is_imported BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0,
  supplier_id BIGINT
);
CREATE TABLE access_logs (
  log_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id BIGINT,

{"orders":{"amount":94.296%,"order_id":100%,"ship_city":0.01%,"ship_country":0.01%},"users":{"user_id":100%,"username":100%}}

## Original SQL:
SELECT 
    o.order_id, 
    o.amount, 
    o.ship_country, 
    o.ship_city, 
    u.user_id, 
    u.username
FROM 
    orders o
INNER JOIN 
    users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id
ORDER BY 
    o.ship_country ASC, 
    o.ship_city ASC;

## Index Recommendation:

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_ship_cover ON orders (ship_country, ship_city, user_id, order_id, amount); Reason: Covering index - the leading columns (ship_country, ship_city) match the ORDER BY so MySQL can read rows in sorted order and eliminate the filesort, while including user_id, order_id, and amount lets the query be satisfied entirely from the index without accessing the orders table.

SELECT 
    u.username, 
    o.order_sn, 
    o.amount, 
    o.pay_time,

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_user_pay ON orders(user_id, pay_time); Reason: Composite index - allows the optimizer to drive from orders by filtering user_id = 12272 and return rows pre-sorted by pay_time, eliminating the full table scan on products and the "Using temporary; Using filesort", while the join to products still uses its PRIMARY key via eq_ref.

SELECT

    (SELECT AVG(o.amount)

model_answers.model_answer: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_user_amount_paytime ON orders(user_id, amount, pay_time); Reason: Covering index - the two dependent subqueries both filter orders by user_id and only read amount and pay_time, so a covering index on (user_id, amount, pay_time) eliminates their full table scans and serves both aggregates directly from the index; the gender filter is not indexed because its selectivity (0.0060%) is far too low to benefit.

CREATE TABLE users (
  user_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  username VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  email VARCHAR(200) UNIQUE,
  phone VARCHAR(20),
  gender TINYINT,
  birthdate DATE,
  register_channel VARCHAR(50),
  last_login DATETIME,
  points INT DEFAULT 0,
  vip_level TINYINT DEFAULT 0
);

  order_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,
  order_sn VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  amount DECIMAL(12, 2) NOT NULL,
  currency CHAR(3) DEFAULT 'CNY',
  pay_time DATETIME,
  ship_country VARCHAR(50),
  ship_city VARCHAR(50),
  ship_zip VARCHAR(20),
  delivery_company VARCHAR(50)
);
CREATE TABLE products (
  product_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  product_name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
  category_id BIGINT NOT NULL,
  brand VARCHAR(100),
  origin_country VARCHAR(50),
  price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
  weight DECIMAL(10, 2),
  stock INT NOT NULL,
  is_imported BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0,
  supplier_id BIGINT
);

  log_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id BIGINT,
  request_url VARCHAR(500),

SELECT u.user_id, u.username, u.email
FROM users u
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM orders o
    WHERE o.user_id = u.user_id

)

CREATE TABLE users (
  user_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  username VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  email VARCHAR(200) UNIQUE,
  phone VARCHAR(20),

  birthdate DATE,

  points INT DEFAULT 0,

) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
CREATE TABLE orders (
  order_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,
  order_sn VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  amount DECIMAL(12, 2) NOT NULL,
  currency CHAR(3) DEFAULT 'CNY',
  pay_time DATETIME,
  ship_country VARCHAR(50),
  ship_city VARCHAR(50),
  phone VARCHAR(20),
  delivery_company VARCHAR(50)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8;

case_datas.mode_question:
You are a database expert specializing in SQL index recommendation.
Given the table definitions, EXPLAIN, column selectivity, and the original SQL query, analyze the performance and provide index recommendations.

## Output Requirements:
Output **only** a single string sentence. Choose one of the following three scenarios:

1. **No performance issue**: If the query performance is acceptable and no index is needed, output: "No performance issues; no indexes need to be created."

2. **Cannot solve by index**: If the performance problem cannot be solved by creating indexes, output a brief one-sentence explanation starting with "Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: " followed by the reason.

3. **Create index**: If indexes can improve performance, output the CREATE INDEX statement(s) followed by a brief reason. You MUST explicitly specify the index type in the reason. Format: "CREATE INDEX statement(s); Reason: [index type] - brief explanation"
   
   Only three index types are allowed:
   - Single-column index: index on one column
   - Composite index: index on multiple columns
   - Covering index: index that includes all columns needed by the query
   
   If multiple indexes are needed, output them separated by semicolons, then add a brief reason at the end.
   Example: "Complete CREATE INDEX statement; Reason: Single-column/Composite/Covering index - brief explanation"

## Table Definitions:
CREATE TABLE users (
  user_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  username VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  email VARCHAR(200) UNIQUE,
  phone VARCHAR(20),
  gender TINYINT,
  birthdate DATE,
  register_channel VARCHAR(50),
  last_login DATETIME,
  points INT DEFAULT 0,
  vip_level TINYINT DEFAULT 0
);

|---|-----------|-----|----------|----|-------------|---|-------|---|------|--------|-----|

{"users":{"birthdate":19.876%,"user_id":100%,"username":100%}}

SELECT user_id,username,birthdate FROM users 
WHERE 1 = 1;

model_answers.model_answer: Unable to solve SQL performance issues by creating indexes. Reason: The query has no filtering condition (WHERE 1=1) and returns all rows, so a full table scan is unavoidable and indexes cannot reduce the rows scanned.

model_answers.model_answer: SELECT s.student_id, s.student_name, c.course_name, e.enrollment_date, t.teacher_name FROM enrollments e JOIN students s ON s.student_id = e.student_id JOIN courses c ON c.course_id = e.course_id LEFT JOIN teachers t ON t.teacher_id = c.department_id LEFT JOIN departments d ON d.department_id = t.department_id AND d.location = 'Building A' WHERE e.enrollment_year = 2024 AND e.enrollment_date BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2024-06-30' AND s.major = 'Physics' AND s.enroll_count > 5 AND c.course_name LIKE 'Advanced %' AND c.course_credits >= 3;

## Table Definitions:
CREATE SEQUENCE DOC_VIEW_HISTORY_SEQ; CREATE TABLE DOC_VIEW_HISTORY (PKID NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, CONTENT_ID NUMBER, CONTENT_TYPE VARCHAR2(200), ACCESS_TIME TIMESTAMP); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER DOC_VIEW_HISTORY_BI BEFORE INSERT ON DOC_VIEW_HISTORY FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.PKID IS NULL THEN SELECT DOC_VIEW_HISTORY_SEQ.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.PKID FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_dvh_content_type ON DOC_VIEW_HISTORY(CONTENT_TYPE);
CREATE INDEX idx_dvh_content_id ON DOC_VIEW_HISTORY(CONTENT_ID);
CREATE INDEX idx_dvh_access_time ON DOC_VIEW_HISTORY(ACCESS_TIME);
CREATE SEQUENCE ARCHIVE_FILE_SEQ; CREATE TABLE ARCHIVE_FILE (REF_ID NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, ARCHIVE_ID NUMBER); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER ARCHIVE_FILE_BI BEFORE INSERT ON ARCHIVE_FILE FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.REF_ID IS NULL THEN SELECT ARCHIVE_FILE_SEQ.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.REF_ID FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_af_archive_id ON ARCHIVE_FILE(ARCHIVE_ID);
CREATE SEQUENCE ARCHIVE_FILE_INFO_SEQ; CREATE TABLE ARCHIVE_FILE_INFO (PKID NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, FILE_TITLE VARCHAR2(200), FILE_CODE VARCHAR2(100), CATEGORY_ID NUMBER, IS_DELETED VARCHAR2(1) DEFAULT '0'); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER ARCHIVE_FILE_INFO_BI BEFORE INSERT ON ARCHIVE_FILE_INFO FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.PKID IS NULL THEN SELECT ARCHIVE_FILE_INFO_SEQ.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.PKID FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_afi_category_id ON ARCHIVE_FILE_INFO(CATEGORY_ID);
CREATE INDEX idx_afi_is_deleted ON ARCHIVE_FILE_INFO(IS_DELETED);
CREATE SEQUENCE ARCHIVE_CATEGORY_INFO_SEQ; CREATE TABLE ARCHIVE_CATEGORY_INFO (PKID NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, TYPE_ID NUMBER); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER ARCHIVE_CATEGORY_INFO_BI BEFORE INSERT ON ARCHIVE_CATEGORY_INFO FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.PKID IS NULL THEN SELECT ARCHIVE_CATEGORY_INFO_SEQ.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.PKID FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_aci_type_id ON ARCHIVE_CATEGORY_INFO(TYPE_ID);

## Original SQL:
SELECT afi.pkid,afi.FILE_TITLE,afi.FILE_CODE fdNo,w.sumNumber countNumber FROM (
  SELECT  max(CONTENT_ID) CONTENT_ID,sum(countNumber) sumNumber ,max(ARCHIVE_ID) ARCHIVE_ID FROM (
  SELECT CONTENT_ID,count(1) countNumber from
  DOC_VIEW_HISTORY WHERE CONTENT_TYPE= 'org.demo.repository.model.RecordEntry' AND  CONTENT_ID IS NOT NULL
  <if test="startDate != null and startDate != ''">
   and ACCESS_TIME >= to_date(#{startDate},'yyyy-mm-dd')
  </if>
  <if test="endDate != null and endDate != ''">
   and ACCESS_TIME <= to_date(#{endDate},'yyyy-mm-dd')
  </if>
  GROUP BY CONTENT_ID ) t LEFT JOIN ARCHIVE_FILE af ON af.REF_ID = t.CONTENT_ID
  GROUP BY ARCHIVE_ID order by  sumNumber desc ) w
  LEFT JOIN ARCHIVE_FILE_INFO afi
  ON afi.PKID = w.ARCHIVE_ID
  left join ARCHIVE_CATEGORY_INFO aci  ON aci.PKID  = afi.CATEGORY_ID
  <where>
   afi.IS_DELETED = '0'
   <if test=" categoryTypeIds != null  and categoryTypeIds.size>0">
  and  aci.TYPE_ID in
  <foreach collection="categoryTypeIds" item="typeId" open="(" separator="," close=")">
  #{typeId}
  </foreach>
   </if>
   and rownum <![CDATA[ <= ]]> 10
  </where>

model_answers.model_answer: SELECT * FROM ( SELECT afi.pkid, afi.FILE_TITLE, afi.FILE_CODE fdNo, w.sumNumber countNumber FROM ( SELECT af.ARCHIVE_ID ARCHIVE_ID, COUNT(1) sumNumber FROM DOC_VIEW_HISTORY t JOIN ARCHIVE_FILE af ON af.REF_ID = t.CONTENT_ID WHERE t.CONTENT_TYPE = 'org.demo.repository.model.RecordEntry' AND t.CONTENT_ID IS NOT NULL <if test="startDate != null and startDate != ''"> and t.ACCESS_TIME >= to_date(#{startDate},'yyyy-mm-dd') </if> <if test="endDate != null and endDate != ''"> and t.ACCESS_TIME <= to_date(#{endDate},'yyyy-mm-dd') </if> GROUP BY af.ARCHIVE_ID ) w JOIN ARCHIVE_FILE_INFO afi ON afi.PKID = w.ARCHIVE_ID AND afi.IS_DELETED = '0' <if test="categoryTypeIds != null and categoryTypeIds.size>0"> JOIN ARCHIVE_CATEGORY_INFO aci ON aci.PKID = afi.CATEGORY_ID AND aci.TYPE_ID in <foreach collection="categoryTypeIds" item="typeId" open="(" separator="," close=")"> #{typeId} </foreach> </if> ORDER BY w.sumNumber DESC ) WHERE rownum <![CDATA[ <= ]]> 10

CREATE SEQUENCE mrtrecordinfo_seq; CREATE TABLE mrtrecordinfo (ID NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, IN_TIMES NUMBER, NAME VARCHAR2(100), IN_DATE DATE, OUT_DATE DATE, OUT_DEPT VARCHAR2(100), RECORD_STATE VARCHAR2(50)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER mrtrecordinfo_bi BEFORE INSERT ON mrtrecordinfo FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.ID IS NULL THEN SELECT mrtrecordinfo_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.ID FROM DUAL; END IF; END;

SELECT A.ID AS id, A.PRAMARYID AS primaryId, A.OPERCODE AS operCode, A.OPERNAME AS operName, A.OPERDEPT AS operDept, A.OPERDATE AS operDate, A.MARK AS mark, C.BACKREASON AS mark1, A.MARK2 AS mark2, A.MARK3 AS mark3, A.MARK4 AS mark4, A.OPERTYPE AS operType, A.NEXTOPERTYPE AS nextOperType, A.PREOPERTYPE AS preOperType, A.VALID AS valid, A.CONFIRMUSER AS confirmUser, A.CONFIRMDEPT AS confirmDept, A.VERSIONNUMBER AS versionNumber, B.IN_TIMES AS inTimes, B.NAME AS patient, B.IN_DATE AS inDate, B.OUT_DATE AS outDate, B.OUT_DEPT AS outDept, B.RECORD_STATE AS recordState FROM mrtopration A LEFT JOIN mrtrecordinfo B ON A.ID = B.ID LEFT JOIN mrtrecordregresses C ON B.ID = C.RECODE_NO AND A.OPERDATE = C.OPER_DATE AND A.OPERCODE = C.OPERID WHERE A.ID IN (SELECT bid FROM (SELECT B.ID AS bid, B.IN_DATE AS inDate, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY B.IN_DATE DESC) AS rn FROM mrtopration A LEFT JOIN mrtrecordinfo B ON A.ID = B.ID WHERE B.ID LIKE :"SYS_B_0" || :1 || :" SYS_B_1" GROUP BY B.ID, B.IN_DATE) WHERE rn BETWEEN :2 + :"SYS_B_2" AND :3 + :4 ) ORDER BY A.OPERDATE DESC

## Optimized SQL:

model_answers.model_answer: SELECT A.ID AS id, A.PRAMARYID AS primaryId, A.OPERCODE AS operCode, A.OPERNAME AS operName, A.OPERDEPT AS operDept, A.OPERDATE AS operDate, A.MARK AS mark, C.BACKREASON AS mark1, A.MARK2 AS mark2, A.MARK3 AS mark3, A.MARK4 AS mark4, A.OPERTYPE AS operType, A.NEXTOPERTYPE AS nextOperType, A.PREOPERTYPE AS preOperType, A.VALID AS valid, A.CONFIRMUSER AS confirmUser, A.CONFIRMDEPT AS confirmDept, A.VERSIONNUMBER AS versionNumber, B.IN_TIMES AS inTimes, B.NAME AS patient, B.IN_DATE AS inDate, B.OUT_DATE AS outDate, B.OUT_DEPT AS outDept, B.RECORD_STATE AS recordState FROM mrtopration A JOIN (SELECT bid FROM (SELECT B.ID AS bid, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY B.IN_DATE DESC) AS rn FROM mrtrecordinfo B WHERE B.ID LIKE :"SYS_B_0" || :1 || :" SYS_B_1") WHERE rn BETWEEN :2 + :"SYS_B_2" AND :3 + :4) D ON A.ID = D.bid LEFT JOIN mrtrecordinfo B ON A.ID = B.ID LEFT JOIN mrtrecordregresses C ON B.ID = C.RECODE_NO AND A.OPERDATE = C.OPER_DATE AND A.OPERCODE = C.OPERID ORDER BY A.OPERDATE DESC

{"case_id": "24", "db_type": "oracle", "description": "иҝҷжҳҜдёҖз§Қз»Ҹе…ёзҡ„UNION ALLж¶ҲйҷӨдјҳеҢ–жҠҖжңҜгҖӮеҪ“UNION ALLзҡ„еӨҡдёӘеҲҶж”ҜжҹҘиҜўзӣёеҗҢзҡ„иЎЁпјҢдё”д»…еңЁSELECTеҲ—иЎЁзҡ„и®Ўз®—йҖ»иҫ‘жҲ–WHEREжқЎд»¶зҡ„еёёйҮҸеҖјдёҠжңүе·®ејӮж—¶пјҢеҸҜд»ҘйҖҡиҝҮCASEиЎЁиҫҫејҸе°ҶдёҚеҗҢзҡ„и®Ўз®—йҖ»иҫ‘з»ҹдёҖеҲ°дёҖдёӘSELECTиҜӯеҸҘдёӯгҖӮж”№еҶҷзҡ„ж ёеҝғжҳҜе°Ҷ'metric_name IN (йӣҶеҗҲ1) иҝ”еӣһround(VALUE,2)'е’Ң'metric_name IN (йӣҶеҗҲ2) иҝ”еӣһround(VALUE*10,2)'дёӨдёӘеҲҶж”ҜпјҢеҗҲе№¶дёә'metric_name IN (йӣҶеҗҲ1+йӣҶеҗҲ2) иҝ”еӣһCASE WHEN...END'гҖӮиҝҷж ·WHEREжқЎд»¶д»ҺдёӨж¬ЎеҲҶеҲ«иҝҮж»ӨеҸҳдёәдёҖж¬ЎиҝҮж»ӨжӣҙеӨ§зҡ„йӣҶеҗҲпјҢSELECTеҲ—иЎЁдҪҝз”ЁCASEж №жҚ®metric_nameеҠЁжҖҒйҖүжӢ©и®Ўз®—ж–№ејҸгҖӮиҝҷз§Қж”№еҶҷеңЁдҝқиҜҒйҖ»иҫ‘зӯүд»·зҡ„еүҚжҸҗдёӢпјҢжҳҫи‘—еҮҸе°‘дәҶI/Oж“ҚдҪңгҖӮ", "difficulty_level": "1", "create_table_statements": ["CREATE SEQUENCE gv_session_seq; CREATE TABLE gv$session (session_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, inst_id NUMBER NOT NULL, TYPE VARCHAR2(20) NOT NULL, status VARCHAR2(20)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER gv_session_bi BEFORE INSERT ON gv$session FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.session_id IS NULL THEN SELECT gv_session_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.session_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;", "CREATE INDEX idx_gv_session_inst_id ON gv$session(inst_id);", "CREATE INDEX idx_gv_session_type_status ON gv$session(TYPE, status);", "CREATE SEQUENCE gv_sysmetric_seq; CREATE TABLE gv$sysmetric (metric_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, inst_id NUMBER NOT NULL, end_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, metric_name VARCHAR2(100) NOT NULL, VALUE NUMBER(20,2), intsize_csec NUMBER); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER gv_sysmetric_bi BEFORE INSERT ON gv$sysmetric FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.metric_id IS NULL THEN SELECT gv_sysmetric_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.metric_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;", "CREATE INDEX idx_gv_sysmetric_inst_id ON gv$sysmetric(inst_id);", "CREATE INDEX idx_gv_sysmetric_intsize ON gv$sysmetric(intsize_csec);", "CREATE INDEX idx_gv_sysmetric_metric_name ON gv$sysmetric(metric_name);"], "sql": "SELECT
  c.inst_id,
  m.end_time AS checktime,
  c.conn,
  c.activeconn,
  m.sqlthroughput,
  m.sqlavgtime,
  m.user_commits,
  m.user_rollbacks,
  m.user_commits + m.user_rollbacks AS transactioncount,
  m.transactionavgtime
FROM
  (
  WITH
   t1 AS (
  SELECT
  inst_id,
  COUNT(*) conn
  FROM
  gv$session
  WHERE
  TYPE = 'USER'
  GROUP BY
  inst_id
   ),
   t2 AS (
  SELECT
  inst_id,
  COUNT(*) activeconn
  FROM
  gv$session
  WHERE
  TYPE = 'USER'
  AND status = 'ACTIVE'
  GROUP BY
  inst_id
   )
  SELECT
   t1.inst_id,
   t1.conn,
   t2.activeconn
  FROM
   t1,
   t2
  WHERE
   t1.inst_id = t2.inst_id
  ) c,
  (
  SELECT
   *
  FROM
   (
  SELECT
  inst_id,
  end_time,
  metric_name,
  round(VALUE, 2) VALUE
  FROM
  gv$sysmetric
  WHERE
  round(intsize_csec / 100, 0) = 60
  AND metric_name IN (
   'Executions Per Sec',
   'User Commits Per Sec',
   'User Rollbacks Per Sec'
  )
  UNION ALL
  SELECT
  inst_id,
  end_time,
  metric_name,
  round(VALUE * 10, 2) VALUE
  FROM
  gv$sysmetric
  WHERE
  round(intsize_csec / 100, 0) = 60
  AND metric_name IN (
   'SQL Service Response Time',
   'Response Time Per Txn'
  )
   ) PIVOT (
  AVG(VALUE) FOR metric_name IN (
  'Executions Per Sec' sqlthroughput,
  'SQL Service Response Time' sqlavgtime,
  'User Commits Per Sec' user_commits,
  'User Rollbacks Per Sec' user_rollbacks,
  'Response Time Per Txn' transactionavgtime
  )
   )
  ) m
WHERE
  c.inst_id = m.inst_id
ORDER BY

## Table Definitions:
CREATE SEQUENCE gv_session_seq; CREATE TABLE gv$session (session_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, inst_id NUMBER NOT NULL, TYPE VARCHAR2(20) NOT NULL, status VARCHAR2(20)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER gv_session_bi BEFORE INSERT ON gv$session FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.session_id IS NULL THEN SELECT gv_session_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.session_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_gv_session_inst_id ON gv$session(inst_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_gv_session_type_status ON gv$session(TYPE, status);
CREATE SEQUENCE gv_sysmetric_seq; CREATE TABLE gv$sysmetric (metric_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, inst_id NUMBER NOT NULL, end_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, metric_name VARCHAR2(100) NOT NULL, VALUE NUMBER(20,2), intsize_csec NUMBER); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER gv_sysmetric_bi BEFORE INSERT ON gv$sysmetric FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.metric_id IS NULL THEN SELECT gv_sysmetric_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.metric_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_gv_sysmetric_inst_id ON gv$sysmetric(inst_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_gv_sysmetric_intsize ON gv$sysmetric(intsize_csec);
CREATE INDEX idx_gv_sysmetric_metric_name ON gv$sysmetric(metric_name);

SELECT
  c.inst_id,
  m.end_time AS checktime,
  c.conn,

  m.sqlthroughput,

  m.user_rollbacks,
  m.user_commits + m.user_rollbacks AS transactioncount,
  m.transactionavgtime
FROM
  (
  WITH
   t1 AS (
  SELECT
  inst_id,
  COUNT(*) conn
  FROM
  gv$session
  WHERE
  TYPE = 'USER'
  GROUP BY
  inst_id
   ),
   t2 AS (
  SELECT
  inst_id,
  COUNT(*) activeconn
  FROM
  gv$session
  WHERE
  TYPE = 'USER'
  AND status = 'ACTIVE'
  GROUP BY
  inst_id
   )
  SELECT
   t1.inst_id,
   t1.conn,
   t2.activeconn
  FROM
   t1,
   t2
  WHERE
   t1.inst_id = t2.inst_id
  ) c,
  (
  SELECT
   *
  FROM
   (
  SELECT
  inst_id,
  end_time,
  metric_name,
  round(VALUE, 2) VALUE
  FROM
  gv$sysmetric
  WHERE
  round(intsize_csec / 100, 0) = 60
  AND metric_name IN (
   'Executions Per Sec',
   'User Commits Per Sec',
   'User Rollbacks Per Sec'
  )
  UNION ALL
  SELECT
  inst_id,
  end_time,
  metric_name,
  round(VALUE * 10, 2) VALUE
  FROM
  gv$sysmetric
  WHERE
  round(intsize_csec / 100, 0) = 60
  AND metric_name IN (
   'SQL Service Response Time',
   'Response Time Per Txn'
  )
   ) PIVOT (
  AVG(VALUE) FOR metric_name IN (
  'Executions Per Sec' sqlthroughput,
  'SQL Service Response Time' sqlavgtime,
  'User Commits Per Sec' user_commits,
  'User Rollbacks Per Sec' user_rollbacks,
  'Response Time Per Txn' transactionavgtime
  )
   )
  ) m
WHERE
  c.inst_id = m.inst_id
ORDER BY
  inst_id

model_answers.model_answer: SELECT c.inst_id, m.end_time AS checktime, c.conn, c.activeconn, m.sqlthroughput, m.sqlavgtime, m.user_commits, m.user_rollbacks, m.user_commits + m.user_rollbacks AS transactioncount, m.transactionavgtime FROM (SELECT inst_id, COUNT(*) conn, COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'ACTIVE' THEN 1 END) activeconn FROM gv$session WHERE TYPE = 'USER' GROUP BY inst_id) c, (SELECT * FROM (SELECT inst_id, end_time, metric_name, CASE WHEN metric_name IN ('SQL Service Response Time', 'Response Time Per Txn') THEN round(VALUE * 10, 2) ELSE round(VALUE, 2) END VALUE FROM gv$sysmetric WHERE intsize_csec >= 5950 AND intsize_csec < 6050 AND metric_name IN ('Executions Per Sec', 'User Commits Per Sec', 'User Rollbacks Per Sec', 'SQL Service Response Time', 'Response Time Per Txn')) PIVOT (AVG(VALUE) FOR metric_name IN ('Executions Per Sec' sqlthroughput, 'SQL Service Response Time' sqlavgtime, 'User Commits Per Sec' user_commits, 'User Rollbacks Per Sec' user_rollbacks, 'Response Time Per Txn' transactionavgtime))) m WHERE c.inst_id = m.inst_id ORDER BY c.inst_id

WITH acctg AS (SELECT A.tenant_id, A.gl_account_id, B.acctg_period, sum(A.base_posted_dr) AS base_posted_dr1, sum(A.base_posted_cr) AS base_posted_cr1 FROM acctg_trans_detail A LEFT JOIN acctg_trans B ON A.master_voucher_id = B.id AND B.tenant_id = 1065587309019136 WHERE A.tenant_id = 1065587309019136 AND (A.voucher_status_enum IS NULL OR A.voucher_status_enum != 'CANCELLED') AND (B.bo_name IS NULL OR B.bo_name != 'DraftAcctgTrans') GROUP BY A.tenant_id, A.gl_account_id, B.acctg_period) SELECT acctg.tenant_id, acctg.gl_account_id, ac.code, acctg.acctg_period, A.base_posted_dr, A.base_posted_cr, acctg.base_posted_dr1, acctg.base_posted_cr1 FROM acctg LEFT JOIN gl_account_balance A ON A.gl_account_id = acctg.gl_account_id AND A.acctg_period = acctg.acctg_period AND A.tenant_id = 1065587309019136 LEFT JOIN gl_account ac ON ac.id = acctg.gl_account_id AND ac.tenant_id = 1065587309019136 WHERE acctg.base_posted_dr1 != ifnull(A.base_posted_dr, 0) OR acctg.base_posted_cr1 != ifnull(A.base_posted_cr, 0) UNION SELECT A.tenant_id, A.gl_account_id, ac.code, A.acctg_period, A.base_posted_dr, A.base_posted_cr, acctg.base_posted_dr1, acctg.base_posted_cr1 FROM gl_account_balance A LEFT JOIN acctg ON acctg.gl_account_id = A.gl_account_id AND acctg.acctg_period = A.acctg_period AND acctg.tenant_id = A.tenant_id LEFT JOIN gl_account ac ON ac.id = A.gl_account_id AND ac.tenant_id = 1065587309019136 WHERE A.tenant_id = 1065587309019136 AND A.acctg_period NOT LIKE '%00' AND (A.base_posted_dr != ifnull(acctg.base_posted_dr1, 0) OR A.base_posted_cr != ifnull(acctg.base_posted_cr1, 0)) LIMIT 50001;

{"case_id": "26", "db_type": "postgresql", "description": "жң¬ж¬Ўж”№еҶҷйҮҮз”ЁдәҶCTEеёёйҮҸжҸҗеҸ–жЁЎејҸпјҢе°Ҷж—¶й—ҙи®Ўз®—йҖ»иҫ‘пјҲfun_addmonthгҖҒto_charзӯүпјүд»ҺиҝһжҺҘжқЎд»¶дёӯжҸҗеҸ–еҲ°CTEдёӯпјҢзҗҶи®әдёҠеҸҜд»ҘйҒҝе…ҚеңЁеҫӘзҺҜдёӯйҮҚеӨҚи®Ўз®—иҝҷдәӣеҮҪж•°гҖӮеҗҢж—¶е°ҶCOALESCEж”№еҶҷдёәжҳҫејҸORжқЎд»¶пјҢеёҢжңӣдјҳеҢ–еҷЁиғҪжӣҙеҘҪең°зҗҶи§Ји°“иҜҚйҖ»иҫ‘гҖӮз„¶иҖҢпјҢиҝҷз§Қж”№еҶҷеҝҪз•ҘдәҶLIMIT 1еҜ№жү§иЎҢи®ЎеҲ’зҡ„еҪұе“ҚпјҡеңЁеҸӘйңҖиҰҒиҝ”еӣһ1иЎҢзҡ„еңәжҷҜдёӢпјҢеҺҹSQLзҡ„"жғ°жҖ§и®Ўз®—"зӯ–з•ҘжӣҙдјҳпјҢеӣ дёәеёёйҮҸеҮҪж•°еҸӘдјҡиў«и°ғз”ЁжһҒе°‘ж¬Ўж•°гҖӮCTEзҡ„зү©еҢ–жҲҗжң¬е’ҢйўқеӨ–зҡ„жү«жҸҸејҖй”ҖеңЁиҝҷз§ҚеңәжҷҜдёӢж— жі•иў«иҠӮзңҒзҡ„и®Ўз®—жҲҗжң¬жүҖжҠөж¶ҲгҖӮ", "difficulty_level": "1", "create_table_statements": ["CREATE TABLE dwd_csi_ac02 (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, aac001 VARCHAR(50), aae140 VARCHAR(10), aac008 VARCHAR(5), aac031 VARCHAR(5), aac030 NUMERIC(8,0), aae041 NUMERIC(6,0), aae042 NUMERIC(6,0));", "CREATE INDEX idx_dwd_csi_ac02_aac001 ON dwd_csi_ac02(aac001);", "CREATE INDEX idx_dwd_csi_ac02_composite ON dwd_csi_ac02(aae140, aac008, aac031, aac030, aae041, aae042);", "CREATE TABLE dwd_hrss_ac99 (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, aac001 VARCHAR(50), aac147 VARCHAR(100));", "CREATE INDEX idx_dwd_hrss_ac99_aac001 ON dwd_hrss_ac99(aac001);", "CREATE INDEX idx_dwd_hrss_ac99_aac147 ON dwd_hrss_ac99(aac147);"], "sql": "select
	'lxzdqdwjf5nys',
	'иҝһз»ӯеңЁеҪ“еүҚеҚ•дҪҚеҸӮдҝқ5е№ҙд»ҘдёҠ',
	c.aac147,
	'жҳҜ',
	'иҝһз»ӯеңЁеҪ“еүҚеҚ•дҪҚеҸӮдҝқ5е№ҙд»ҘдёҠ'
from
	dwd_csi_ac02 a,
	(
	select
		(fun_addmonth(to_char(current_timestamp, 'yyyymm')::varchar,-61)|| '31')::numeric aac030,
		 fun_addmonth(to_char(current_timestamp, 'yyyymm')::varchar,-61)::numeric aae041,
		to_char(current_timestamp, 'yyyymm')::numeric aae042) b,
	dwd_hrss_ac99 c
where a.aac001::varchar = c.aac001::varchar
	and a.aae140 = '110'
	and a.aac008 = '1'
	and a.aac031 = '1'
	and a.aac030 <= b.aac030
	and a.aae041 <= b.aae041
	and coalesce(a.aae042, 999912) >= b.aae042
	and c.aac147 is not null limit 1", "explain": "QUERY PLAN
Limit  (cost=0.42..3.47 rows=1 width=166)

  ->  Result  (cost=0.00..0.54 rows=1 width=96)

	c.aac147,
	'жҳҜ',
	'иҝһз»ӯеңЁеҪ“еүҚеҚ•дҪҚеҸӮдҝқ5е№ҙд»ҘдёҠ'
from
	dwd_csi_ac02 a,
	(
	select

	dwd_hrss_ac99 c
where a.aac001::varchar = c.aac001::varchar

	and a.aac031 = '1'
	and a.aac030 <= b.aac030

model_answers.model_answer: WITH temp AS (SELECT * FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ON (member_num) member_num, balance, created_on FROM cash_transaction WHERE cash_type = 2 ORDER BY member_num, id DESC) t WHERE balance <> 0) SELECT * FROM temp a FULL OUTER JOIN (SELECT * FROM cash WHERE credit_available <> 0) b ON a.member_num = b.member_num WHERE a.balance <> b.credit_available OR a.member_num IS NULL OR b.member_num IS NULL

CREATE TABLE audit_detail (audd_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, audd_audh_seqno NUMBER NOT NULL, audd_col_sufix VARCHAR2(50), audd_old_value VARCHAR2(200), audd_new_value VARCHAR2(200)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER audit_detail_bi BEFORE INSERT ON audit_detail FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.audd_id IS NULL THEN SELECT audit_detail_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.audd_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;", "CREATE INDEX idx_audd_audh_seqno ON audit_detail(audd_audh_seqno);", "CREATE INDEX idx_audd_col_sufix ON audit_detail(audd_col_sufix);", "CREATE SEQUENCE account_links_seq; CREATE TABLE ACCOUNT_LINKS (accl_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, accl_account_no VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, accl_subacc_no VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, accl_holder_pos NUMBER, accl_cust_seqno NUMBER); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER account_links_bi BEFORE INSERT ON ACCOUNT_LINKS FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.accl_id IS NULL THEN SELECT account_links_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.accl_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;", "CREATE INDEX idx_accl_account_subacc ON ACCOUNT_LINKS(accl_account_no, accl_subacc_no);", "CREATE INDEX idx_accl_holder_pos ON ACCOUNT_LINKS(accl_holder_pos);", "CREATE SEQUENCE account_holding_tax_seq; CREATE TABLE SUMMIT.ACCOUNT_HOLDING_TAX (acctax_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, ACCTAX_ACCOUNT_NO VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, ACCTAX_SUBACC_NO VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, ACCTAX_CUST_SEQNO NUMBER, ACCTAX_EFF_DATE_FROM DATE); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER account_holding_tax_bi BEFORE INSERT ON SUMMIT.ACCOUNT_HOLDING_TAX FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.acctax_id IS NULL THEN SELECT account_holding_tax_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.acctax_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;", "CREATE INDEX idx_acctax_composite ON SUMMIT.ACCOUNT_HOLDING_TAX(ACCTAX_ACCOUNT_NO, ACCTAX_SUBACC_NO, ACCTAX_CUST_SEQNO);"], "sql": "SELECT DISTINCT acc_account_no, 
  acc_subacc_no, 
  acch_title,
  to_char(acc_incep_date,'DD/MM/YYYY') acc_incep_date,
  htax.ACCTAX_EFF_DATE_FROM,
  to_char(onhold_accounts.audh_date, 'DD-MON-YYYY') approved_date,
  act_long_name, 
  tran_amount_cr,
  tran_create_by,
  CASE WHEN (trunc(acc_incep_date) - trunc(onhold_accounts.audh_date)) < 0 THEN 0 ELSE
   (trunc(acc_incep_date) - trunc(onhold_accounts.audh_date)) END time_on_approved,
  acch_brn_code
  FROM
  (
   (
  SELECT accounts.acc_account_no,
   acch_title, 
   accounts.acc_subacc_no,
   acc_incep_date,
   act_long_name,
   tran_amount_cr,
   transactions.tran_create_by,
   first_tran_date,
   acch_brn_code
  FROM accounts 
  JOIN account_headers ON account_headers.acch_account_no = accounts.acc_account_no
  JOIN account_types ON acc_act_code = act_code
  JOIN 
   (
  SELECT tran_account_no, tran_subacc_no, min(tran_seqno) first_tran_seq, min(tran_date) first_tran_date
  FROM transactions
  WHERE tran_amount_cr IS NOT NULL
  AND tran_trans_code IN ('RIC','RSAV')
  AND tran_date BETWEEN IN_FUND_FROM_DATE AND IN_FUND_TO_DATE
  AND tran_pay_method not in ('C','CQ')   --Exclude cash and cheque receipts as those accounts will already have the passbook.
  GROUP BY tran_account_no, tran_subacc_no
   ) first_trans
   ON tran_account_no = acc_account_no AND tran_subacc_no = acc_subacc_no
  JOIN transactions ON tran_seqno = first_trans.first_tran_seq
  WHERE acc_gact_type IN ('S', 'IC') 
   AND acc_incep_date BETWEEN IN_INCEP_FROM_DATE and IN_INCEP_TO_DATE
   ) opened_accounts
   JOIN
   (
  SELECT audh_key1, audh_key2, audh_date, audd_old_value, audd_new_value
  FROM audit_header
  JOIN audit_detail
   ON audh_seqno = audd_audh_seqno
  WHERE audh_table_prefix = 'ACC'
   AND audd_col_sufix = 'PROCESS_STATUS'
   AND audh_change_type  = 'I'
   AND audd_old_value IS NULL 
   AND audd_new_value = 1
   ) onhold_accounts
  ON opened_accounts.acc_account_no = onhold_accounts.audh_key1
   AND opened_accounts.acc_subacc_no = onhold_accounts.audh_key2
   JOIN ACCOUNT_LINKS al ON al.accl_account_no = opened_accounts.ACC_ACCOUNT_NO and al.accl_subacc_no = opened_accounts.ACC_SUBACC_NO and al.accl_holder_pos = 1
   JOIN SUMMIT.ACCOUNT_HOLDING_TAX htax 
  ON htax.ACCTAX_ACCOUNT_NO = al.ACCL_ACCOUNT_NO
   AND htax.ACCTAX_SUBACC_NO = al.ACCL_SUBACC_NO
   AND htax.ACCTAX_CUST_SEQNO = al.ACCL_CUST_SEQNO

Output only the optimized SQL as a single-line statement with no markdown or extra text.

## Table Definitions:
CREATE SEQUENCE accounts_seq; CREATE TABLE accounts (acc_account_no VARCHAR2(50), acc_subacc_no VARCHAR2(50), acc_incep_date DATE NOT NULL, acc_act_code VARCHAR2(20), acc_gact_type VARCHAR2(10), PRIMARY KEY (acc_account_no, acc_subacc_no)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER accounts_bi BEFORE INSERT ON accounts FOR EACH ROW BEGIN NULL; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_accounts_act_code ON accounts(acc_act_code);
CREATE INDEX idx_accounts_gact_type ON accounts(acc_gact_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_accounts_incep_date ON accounts(acc_incep_date);
CREATE SEQUENCE account_headers_seq; CREATE TABLE account_headers (acch_account_no VARCHAR2(50) PRIMARY KEY, acch_title VARCHAR2(200), acch_brn_code VARCHAR2(20)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER account_headers_bi BEFORE INSERT ON account_headers FOR EACH ROW BEGIN NULL; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_acch_account_no ON account_headers(acch_account_no);
CREATE SEQUENCE account_types_seq; CREATE TABLE account_types (act_code VARCHAR2(20) PRIMARY KEY, act_long_name VARCHAR2(100)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER account_types_bi BEFORE INSERT ON account_types FOR EACH ROW BEGIN NULL; END;
CREATE SEQUENCE transactions_seq; CREATE TABLE transactions (tran_seqno NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, tran_account_no VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, tran_subacc_no VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, tran_date DATE NOT NULL, tran_amount_cr NUMBER(18,2), tran_trans_code VARCHAR2(20), tran_pay_method VARCHAR2(10), tran_create_by VARCHAR2(50)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER transactions_bi BEFORE INSERT ON transactions FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.tran_seqno IS NULL THEN SELECT transactions_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.tran_seqno FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_trans_account_subacc ON transactions(tran_account_no, tran_subacc_no);
CREATE INDEX idx_trans_date ON transactions(tran_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_trans_trans_code ON transactions(tran_trans_code);
CREATE INDEX idx_trans_pay_method ON transactions(tran_pay_method);
CREATE SEQUENCE audit_header_seq; CREATE TABLE audit_header (audh_seqno NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, audh_key1 VARCHAR2(50), audh_key2 VARCHAR2(50), audh_date DATE, audh_table_prefix VARCHAR2(20), audh_change_type VARCHAR2(10)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER audit_header_bi BEFORE INSERT ON audit_header FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.audh_seqno IS NULL THEN SELECT audit_header_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.audh_seqno FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_audh_keys ON audit_header(audh_key1, audh_key2);
CREATE INDEX idx_audh_table_prefix ON audit_header(audh_table_prefix);
CREATE INDEX idx_audh_change_type ON audit_header(audh_change_type);
CREATE SEQUENCE audit_detail_seq; CREATE TABLE audit_detail (audd_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, audd_audh_seqno NUMBER NOT NULL, audd_col_sufix VARCHAR2(50), audd_old_value VARCHAR2(200), audd_new_value VARCHAR2(200)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER audit_detail_bi BEFORE INSERT ON audit_detail FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.audd_id IS NULL THEN SELECT audit_detail_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.audd_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_audd_audh_seqno ON audit_detail(audd_audh_seqno);
CREATE INDEX idx_audd_col_sufix ON audit_detail(audd_col_sufix);
CREATE SEQUENCE account_links_seq; CREATE TABLE ACCOUNT_LINKS (accl_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, accl_account_no VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, accl_subacc_no VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, accl_holder_pos NUMBER, accl_cust_seqno NUMBER); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER account_links_bi BEFORE INSERT ON ACCOUNT_LINKS FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.accl_id IS NULL THEN SELECT account_links_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.accl_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_accl_account_subacc ON ACCOUNT_LINKS(accl_account_no, accl_subacc_no);
CREATE INDEX idx_accl_holder_pos ON ACCOUNT_LINKS(accl_holder_pos);
CREATE SEQUENCE account_holding_tax_seq; CREATE TABLE SUMMIT.ACCOUNT_HOLDING_TAX (acctax_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, ACCTAX_ACCOUNT_NO VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, ACCTAX_SUBACC_NO VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, ACCTAX_CUST_SEQNO NUMBER, ACCTAX_EFF_DATE_FROM DATE); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER account_holding_tax_bi BEFORE INSERT ON SUMMIT.ACCOUNT_HOLDING_TAX FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.acctax_id IS NULL THEN SELECT account_holding_tax_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.acctax_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_acctax_composite ON SUMMIT.ACCOUNT_HOLDING_TAX(ACCTAX_ACCOUNT_NO, ACCTAX_SUBACC_NO, ACCTAX_CUST_SEQNO);

## Original SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT acc_account_no, 
  acc_subacc_no, 
  acch_title,
  to_char(acc_incep_date,'DD/MM/YYYY') acc_incep_date,
  htax.ACCTAX_EFF_DATE_FROM,
  to_char(onhold_accounts.audh_date, 'DD-MON-YYYY') approved_date,
  act_long_name, 
  tran_amount_cr,
  tran_create_by,
  CASE WHEN (trunc(acc_incep_date) - trunc(onhold_accounts.audh_date)) < 0 THEN 0 ELSE
   (trunc(acc_incep_date) - trunc(onhold_accounts.audh_date)) END time_on_approved,
  acch_brn_code
  FROM
  (
   (
  SELECT accounts.acc_account_no,
   acch_title, 
   accounts.acc_subacc_no,
   acc_incep_date,
   act_long_name,
   tran_amount_cr,
   transactions.tran_create_by,
   first_tran_date,
   acch_brn_code
  FROM accounts 
  JOIN account_headers ON account_headers.acch_account_no = accounts.acc_account_no
  JOIN account_types ON acc_act_code = act_code
  JOIN 
   (
  SELECT tran_account_no, tran_subacc_no, min(tran_seqno) first_tran_seq, min(tran_date) first_tran_date
  FROM transactions
  WHERE tran_amount_cr IS NOT NULL
  AND tran_trans_code IN ('RIC','RSAV')
  AND tran_date BETWEEN IN_FUND_FROM_DATE AND IN_FUND_TO_DATE
  AND tran_pay_method not in ('C','CQ')   --Exclude cash and cheque receipts as those accounts will already have the passbook.
  GROUP BY tran_account_no, tran_subacc_no
   ) first_trans
   ON tran_account_no = acc_account_no AND tran_subacc_no = acc_subacc_no
  JOIN transactions ON tran_seqno = first_trans.first_tran_seq
  WHERE acc_gact_type IN ('S', 'IC') 
   AND acc_incep_date BETWEEN IN_INCEP_FROM_DATE and IN_INCEP_TO_DATE
   ) opened_accounts
   JOIN
   (
  SELECT audh_key1, audh_key2, audh_date, audd_old_value, audd_new_value
  FROM audit_header
  JOIN audit_detail
   ON audh_seqno = audd_audh_seqno
  WHERE audh_table_prefix = 'ACC'
   AND audd_col_sufix = 'PROCESS_STATUS'
   AND audh_change_type  = 'I'
   AND audd_old_value IS NULL 
   AND audd_new_value = 1
   ) onhold_accounts
  ON opened_accounts.acc_account_no = onhold_accounts.audh_key1
   AND opened_accounts.acc_subacc_no = onhold_accounts.audh_key2
   JOIN ACCOUNT_LINKS al ON al.accl_account_no = opened_accounts.ACC_ACCOUNT_NO and al.accl_subacc_no = opened_accounts.ACC_SUBACC_NO and al.accl_holder_pos = 1
   JOIN SUMMIT.ACCOUNT_HOLDING_TAX htax 
  ON htax.ACCTAX_ACCOUNT_NO = al.ACCL_ACCOUNT_NO
   AND htax.ACCTAX_SUBACC_NO = al.ACCL_SUBACC_NO
   AND htax.ACCTAX_CUST_SEQNO = al.ACCL_CUST_SEQNO
  )

trunc(onhold_accounts.audh_date)) < 0 THEN 0 ELSE (trunc(acc_incep_date) - trunc(onhold_accounts.audh_date)) END time_on_approved, acch_brn_code FROM ( SELECT accounts.acc_account_no, acch_title, accounts.acc_subacc_no, acc_incep_date, act_long_name, tran_amount_cr, transactions.tran_create_by, first_tran_date, acch_brn_code FROM accounts JOIN account_headers ON account_headers.acch_account_no = accounts.acc_account_no JOIN account_types ON acc_act_code = act_code JOIN ( SELECT tran_account_no, tran_subacc_no, min(tran_seqno) first_tran_seq, min(tran_date) first_tran_date FROM transactions WHERE tran_amount_cr IS NOT NULL AND tran_trans_code IN ('RIC','RSAV') AND tran_date BETWEEN IN_FUND_FROM_DATE AND IN_FUND_TO_DATE AND tran_pay_method NOT IN ('C','CQ') GROUP BY tran_account_no, tran_subacc_no ) first_trans ON tran_account_no = acc_account_no AND tran_subacc_no = acc_subacc_no JOIN transactions ON tran_seqno = first_trans.first_tran_seq WHERE acc_gact_type IN ('S','IC') AND acc_incep_date BETWEEN IN_INCEP_FROM_DATE AND IN_INCEP_TO_DATE ) opened_accounts JOIN ( SELECT audh_key1, audh_key2, audh_date FROM audit_header WHERE audh_table_prefix = 'ACC' AND audh_change_type = 'I' AND EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM audit_detail WHERE audd_audh_seqno = audh_seqno AND audd_col_sufix = 'PROCESS_STATUS' AND audd_old_value IS NULL AND audd_new_value = 1 ) ) onhold_accounts ON opened_accounts.acc_account_no = onhold_accounts.audh_key1 AND opened_accounts.acc_subacc_no = onhold_accounts.audh_key2 JOIN ACCOUNT_LINKS al ON al.accl_account_no = opened_accounts.ACC_ACCOUNT_NO AND al.accl_subacc_no = opened_accounts.ACC_SUBACC_NO AND al.accl_holder_pos = 1 JOIN SUMMIT.ACCOUNT_HOLDING_TAX htax ON htax.ACCTAX_ACCOUNT_NO = al.ACCL_ACCOUNT_NO AND htax.ACCTAX_SUBACC_NO = al.ACCL_SUBACC_NO AND htax.ACCTAX_CUST_SEQNO = al.ACCL_CUST_SEQNO

  n6.nortran_value "END JOB EFFECTIVE DATE",
  n7.nortran_value "END PERSONNEL DATE"
   ,case b.nobtran_trans_status_ind

   when 'C' then 'Completed'
   when 'V' then 'Voided' 
   when 'D' then 'Disapproved'
   when 'R' then 'Return for Correction'
   when 'P' then 'Pending'
   when 'W' then 'Waiting'
   when 'T' then 'Partially Completed'
   when 'N' then 'Cancelled'
   when 'U' then 'Superuser Update'
  end trans_status   
  ,case r.norrout_queue_status_ind
   when 'A' then 'Approved by that Approver'
   when 'D' then 'Disapproved'
   when 'F' then 'FYI'
   when 'I' then 'In Queue'
   when 'K' then 'Acknowledged'
   when 'L' then 'Applied by Applier'
   when 'M' then 'More Information'
   when 'O' then 'Overridden'
   when 'P' then 'Pending Action by Approver/Applier '
   when 'Q' then 'Removed from Queue'
   when 'R' then 'Return for Correction'
  end queue_status
  ,r.norrout_level_no
  

  from nobtran b,spriden s, norrout r, pebempl pp, /*nbrjobs nbr,*/
  (select nc.nortran_transaction_no,nc.nortran_apty_code,nc.nortran_aufd_code,nc.nortran_value
  from nortran nc
   where nc.nortran_aufd_code = 'PEBEMPL_CURRENT_HIRE_DATE'
  ) n1, /* Current Hire Date */
  (select nb.nortran_transaction_no,nb.nortran_apty_code,nb.nortran_aufd_code,nb.nortran_value
  from nortran nb
   where nb.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_PERS_CHG_DATE' 
  ) n2,  /*Begin Personnnel Date*/ 
  (select ns.nortran_transaction_no,ns.nortran_apty_code,ns.nortran_aufd_code,ns.nortran_value
  from nortran ns
   where ns.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_SGRP_CODE'
   ) n3, /* Salary Group */
  (select na.nortran_transaction_no,na.nortran_apty_code,na.nortran_aufd_code,na.nortran_value
  from nortran na
   where na.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_APPT_PCT'
  ) n4, /*Appointment Percent*/ 
  (select nu.nortran_transaction_no,nu.nortran_apty_code,nu.nortran_aufd_code,nu.nortran_value
  from nortran nu
   where nu.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_ANN_SALARY'
  ) n5, /*Annual Salary*/
  (select nj.nortran_transaction_no,nj.nortran_apty_code,nj.nortran_aufd_code,nj.nortran_value
  from nortran nj
   where nj.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_EFFECTIVE_DATE'  
  and nj.nortran_apty_code in ('CFOAP','NWHIR','NWTMP','ADCOMP','JBTERM')) n6,  /*End Job Effective Date*/
  (select np.nortran_transaction_no,np.nortran_apty_code,np.nortran_aufd_code,np.nortran_value
  from nortran np
   where np.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_PERS_CHG_DATE'  
  and np.nortran_apty_code in ('CFOAP','NWHIR','NWTMP','ADCOMP','JBTERM')) n7,  /*End Personnel Date*/

  (select t.nortern_transaction_no,t.nortern_effective_date,t.nortern_earn_code,
   t.nortern_hrs, t.nortern_special_rate
  from nortern t) t1 

   ,(select unique zz.nortran_transaction_no,zz.nortran_posn
  from nortran zz
   where zz.nortran_posn is not null
  ) z

 where b.nobtran_acat_code in ('CFOAP','NWHIR','NWTMP','ADCOMP','JBTERM') /*employee*/
  /*waiting,completed,partially completedc,ancelled,supperuser update */
  /* and nobtran_trans_status_ind in ('W','C','T','U') */

case_datas.mode_question:
You are a database expert in SQL performance optimization.
Given the table definitions, explain output and the original SQL, apply appropriate optimizations.
Output only the optimized SQL as a single-line statement with no markdown or extra text.

## Table Definitions:
CREATE SEQUENCE nobtran_seq; CREATE TABLE nobtran (nobtran_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, nobtran_transaction_no NUMBER NOT NULL, nobtran_acat_code VARCHAR2(20), nobtran_effective_date DATE, nobtran_trans_status_ind VARCHAR2(1), nobtran_pidm NUMBER NOT NULL, nobtran_apply_ind VARCHAR2(1) DEFAULT 'N'); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER nobtran_bi BEFORE INSERT ON nobtran FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.nobtran_id IS NULL THEN SELECT nobtran_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.nobtran_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_nobtran_acat_code ON nobtran(nobtran_acat_code);
CREATE INDEX idx_nobtran_pidm ON nobtran(nobtran_pidm);
CREATE INDEX idx_nobtran_transaction_no ON nobtran(nobtran_transaction_no);
CREATE INDEX idx_nobtran_status ON nobtran(nobtran_trans_status_ind);
CREATE INDEX idx_nobtran_apply_ind ON nobtran(nobtran_apply_ind);
CREATE SEQUENCE spriden_seq; CREATE TABLE spriden (spriden_pidm NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, spriden_id VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, spriden_last_name VARCHAR2(100) NOT NULL, spriden_first_name VARCHAR2(100), spriden_change_ind VARCHAR2(1)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER spriden_bi BEFORE INSERT ON spriden FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.spriden_pidm IS NULL THEN SELECT spriden_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.spriden_pidm FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_spriden_change_ind ON spriden(spriden_change_ind);
CREATE SEQUENCE norrout_seq; CREATE TABLE norrout (norrout_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, norrout_transaction_no NUMBER NOT NULL, norrout_queue_status_ind VARCHAR2(1), norrout_level_no NUMBER); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER norrout_bi BEFORE INSERT ON norrout FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.norrout_id IS NULL THEN SELECT norrout_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.norrout_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_norrout_transaction_no ON norrout(norrout_transaction_no);
CREATE INDEX idx_norrout_level_no ON norrout(norrout_level_no);
CREATE INDEX idx_norrout_queue_status ON norrout(norrout_queue_status_ind);
CREATE SEQUENCE pebempl_seq; CREATE TABLE pebempl (pebempl_pidm NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, pebempl_ecls_code VARCHAR2(10) NOT NULL, pebempl_current_hire_date DATE); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER pebempl_bi BEFORE INSERT ON pebempl FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.pebempl_pidm IS NULL THEN SELECT pebempl_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.pebempl_pidm FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_pebempl_ecls_code ON pebempl(pebempl_ecls_code);
CREATE SEQUENCE nortran_seq; CREATE TABLE nortran (nortran_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, nortran_transaction_no NUMBER NOT NULL, nortran_apty_code VARCHAR2(20), nortran_aufd_code VARCHAR2(50) NOT NULL, nortran_value VARCHAR2(200), nortran_posn VARCHAR2(50)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER nortran_bi BEFORE INSERT ON nortran FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.nortran_id IS NULL THEN SELECT nortran_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.nortran_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_nortran_transaction_no ON nortran(nortran_transaction_no);
CREATE INDEX idx_nortran_aufd_code ON nortran(nortran_aufd_code);
CREATE INDEX idx_nortran_apty_code ON nortran(nortran_apty_code);
CREATE SEQUENCE nortern_seq; CREATE TABLE nortern (nortern_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY, nortern_transaction_no NUMBER NOT NULL, nortern_effective_date DATE, nortern_earn_code VARCHAR2(20), nortern_hrs NUMBER(10,2), nortern_special_rate NUMBER(15,2)); CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER nortern_bi BEFORE INSERT ON nortern FOR EACH ROW BEGIN IF :NEW.nortern_id IS NULL THEN SELECT nortern_seq.NEXTVAL INTO :NEW.nortern_id FROM DUAL; END IF; END;
CREATE INDEX idx_nortern_transaction_no ON nortern(nortern_transaction_no);

'Acknowledged' when 'L' then 'Applied by Applier' when 'M' then 'More Information' when 'O' then 'Overridden' when 'P' then 'Pending Action by Approver/Applier ' when 'Q' then 'Removed from Queue' when 'R' then 'Return for Correction' end queue_status, r.norrout_level_no from nobtran b join spriden s on b.nobtran_pidm = s.spriden_pidm and s.spriden_change_ind is null join pebempl pp on s.spriden_pidm = pp.pebempl_pidm and pp.pebempl_ecls_code not in ('RE','FW','GS','MW') join norrout r on b.nobtran_transaction_no = r.norrout_transaction_no and r.norrout_level_no = 90 and r.norrout_queue_status_ind <> 'A' join (select distinct zz.nortran_transaction_no, zz.nortran_posn from nortran zz where zz.nortran_posn is not null) z on b.nobtran_transaction_no = z.nortran_transaction_no left join nortran n1 on b.nobtran_transaction_no = n1.nortran_transaction_no and n1.nortran_aufd_code = 'PEBEMPL_CURRENT_HIRE_DATE' left join nortran n2 on b.nobtran_transaction_no = n2.nortran_transaction_no and n2.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_PERS_CHG_DATE' left join nortran n3 on b.nobtran_transaction_no = n3.nortran_transaction_no and n3.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_SGRP_CODE' left join nortran n4 on b.nobtran_transaction_no = n4.nortran_transaction_no and n4.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_APPT_PCT' left join nortran n5 on b.nobtran_transaction_no = n5.nortran_transaction_no and n5.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_ANN_SALARY' left join nortran n6 on b.nobtran_transaction_no = n6.nortran_transaction_no and n6.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_EFFECTIVE_DATE' and n6.nortran_apty_code in ('CFOAP','NWHIR','NWTMP','ADCOMP','JBTERM') left join nortran n7 on b.nobtran_transaction_no = n7.nortran_transaction_no and n7.nortran_aufd_code = 'NBRJOBS_PERS_CHG_DATE' and n7.nortran_apty_code in ('CFOAP','NWHIR','NWTMP','ADCOMP','JBTERM') left join nortern t1 on b.nobtran_transaction_no = t1.nortern_transaction_no where b.nobtran_acat_code in ('CFOAP','NWHIR','NWTMP','ADCOMP','JBTERM') and b.nobtran_apply_ind = 'N' and b.nobtran_trans_status_ind <> 'V' order by b.nobtran_transaction_no

{"case_id": "31", "db_type": "oracle", "description": "жң¬ж¬Ўж”№еҶҷжҳҜдёҖдёӘзәҜиҜӯжі•еұӮйқўзҡ„еҸҳжӣҙпјҢд»…е°Ҷжҙҫз”ҹиЎЁпјҲеӯҗжҹҘиҜўпјүзҡ„еҲ«еҗҚд»Һ't'ж”№дёә't2'пјҢе№¶зӣёеә”жӣҙж–°еӨ–еұӮSELECTиҜӯеҸҘдёӯзҡ„еҲ—еј•з”ЁпјҲt.ageвҶ’t2.ageзӯүпјүгҖӮиҝҷз§Қж”№еҶҷдёҚж”№еҸҳSQLзҡ„иҜӯд№үгҖҒжү§иЎҢйҖ»иҫ‘жҲ–ж•°жҚ®и®ҝй—®и·Ҝеҫ„пјҢеҜ№ж•°жҚ®еә“дјҳеҢ–еҷЁжқҘиҜҙдёӨдёӘжҹҘиҜўе®Ңе…Ёзӯүд»·гҖӮд»ҺжҖ§иғҪдјҳеҢ–и§’еәҰзңӢпјҢиҝҷдёҚжҳҜдёҖдёӘжңүж•Ҳзҡ„ж”№еҶҷж–№жЎҲпјҢеӣ дёәе®ғдёҚж¶үеҸҠд»»дҪ•иғҪеӨҹжҸҗеҚҮжҖ§иғҪзҡ„жҠҖжңҜжүӢж®өпјҢеҰӮзҙўеј•еҲ©з”ЁгҖҒиҝһжҺҘйЎәеәҸи°ғж•ҙгҖҒи°“иҜҚдёӢжҺЁгҖҒеӯҗжҹҘиҜўеұ•ејҖзӯүгҖӮ", "difficulty_level": "2", "create_table_statements": ["CREATE TABLE SAPR3.BSIS (MANDT VARCHAR2(3) NOT NULL, BUKRS VARCHAR2(4) NOT NULL, BELNR VARCHAR2(10) NOT NULL, GJAHR VARCHAR2(4) NOT NULL, BUZEI VARCHAR2(3) NOT NULL, HKONT VARCHAR2(10), DMBTR NUMBER(15,2), WAERS VARCHAR2(3), MONAT VARCHAR2(2), BLDAT VARCHAR2(8), CONSTRAINT pk_bsis PRIMARY KEY (MANDT, BUKRS, BELNR, GJAHR, BUZEI));", "CREATE INDEX idx_bsis_query ON SAPR3.BSIS(GJAHR, BUKRS, HKONT, MANDT, BUZEI, WAERS, MONAT, BLDAT);", "CREATE INDEX idx_bsis_join ON SAPR3.BSIS(BELNR, GJAHR, BUKRS);", "CREATE TABLE SAPR3.BKPF (MANDT VARCHAR2(3) NOT NULL, BUKRS VARCHAR2(4) NOT NULL, BELNR VARCHAR2(10) NOT NULL, GJAHR VARCHAR2(4) NOT NULL, BLDAT VARCHAR2(8), CONSTRAINT pk_bkpf PRIMARY KEY (MANDT, BUKRS, BELNR, GJAHR));"], "sql": "SELECT
  t.age,
  CASE 
  WHEN t.age BETWEEN 0 AND 3 THEN '0-3 Days'
  WHEN t.age BETWEEN 4 AND 7 THEN '3-7 Days'
  ELSE 'Above 7 Days'
  END AS age_bucket,
  t.amount_local,
  t.hkont AS gl_account,
  t.bukrs AS company_code
FROM (
  SELECT 
  b.BUKRS,
  b.HKONT,
  b.DMBTR AS amount_local,
  (TRUNC(SYSDATE) - TO_DATE(k.BLDAT,'YYYYMMDD')) AS age
  FROM (
  SELECT /*+ FIRST_ROWS(30) */
   BELNR, GJAHR, BUKRS, HKONT, DMBTR
  FROM SAPR3.BSIS
  WHERE GJAHR = '2025'
  AND BUKRS = '0100'
  AND HKONT = '0000895200'
  AND MANDT = '500'
  AND BUZEI = '002'
  AND WAERS = 'INR'
  AND MONAT = '01'
  AND BLDAT = '20240401'
  ) b
  JOIN SAPR3.BKPF k
   ON b.BELNR = k.BELNR
  AND b.GJAHR = k.GJAHR
  AND b.BUKRS = k.BUKRS
) t
ORDER BY t.age;", "explain": "ID	PLAN_STEP	COST	CARDINALITY	BYTES	CPU_COST	IO_COST
